docs.title: Welcome to My Portfolio
docs.text:
Hello! I am Islam Azaizia, and this is my portfolio website created for the "Programming in Python 2026" course.

Use the navigation menu to explore my background, technical skills, and detailed reports on my laboratory works.

This site is statically generated using MkDocs and the Material theme, and it is hosted on GitHub Pages.

docs.title: About the Author
docs.text: Name: Islam Azaizia Role: Software Engineering Student University: ITMO University

docs.title: Background
docs.text: I am a dedicated Software Engineering student at ITMO University in St. Petersburg, with a strong passion for building intelligent, user-centric applications. With a solid foundation in Java and Python, I specialize in transforming complex problems into seamless digital experiences.

docs.title: What I Do
docs.text:
My work lies at the intersection of: - Robust Backend Development - Intuitive UI/UX Design - Artificial Intelligence

I balance my professional work with academic studies, which has equipped me with strong time-management skills and an adaptable, problem-solving mindset. I am always eager to embrace new technologies and push the boundaries of what I can create.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
I am constantly building and scaling independent projects, including: - Interview Coder - iMuslimRU - iDIDDY - iPlam

Whether I am integrating AI features to enhance functionality or designing clean, accessible interfaces, my goal is always to deliver impactful software.

docs.title: Laboratory Work 1
docs.text: Theme: Creating and deploying a static website using MkDocs and publishing on GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 1. Goal of the Work
docs.text:
• Master the process of creating a static site using the MkDocs documentation generator.
  • Learn how to structure project documentation (laboratory portfolio).
  • Study the basic principles of working with Git version control and GitHub.
  • Deploy a static website using GitHub Pages on a username.github.io domain.
  • Master the basic configuration of themes and the mkdocs.yml file.

docs.title: 2. Theoretical Background
docs.text: MkDocs is a fast, simple and downright gorgeous static site generator that's geared towards building project documentation. Documentation source files are written in Markdown, and configured with a single YAML configuration file.

docs.title: 3. Tasks
docs.text:
1. Create a public GitHub repository.
  2. Initialize MkDocs project.
  3. Configure mkdocs.yml and mkdocs-material theme.
  4. Add content pages (About, Labs, etc.).
  5. Build the website locally.
  6. Publish to GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 4. Implementation
docs.text:
The main configuration mkdocs.yml was created with material theme and custom navigation.

theme:
  name: material

A Python virtual environment was created to isolate dependencies. Git and GitHub Pages were configured appropriately to deploy from /docs folder on main branch.

docs.title: 5. Conclusion
docs.text: Successfully generated a static website from Markdown files and deployed it to GitHub Pages. All required structural elements and theme configurations were applied.
